summary: Completed static QA review for Firebase integration in Flutter app (runtime Flutter tests not possible in this container). Verified Firebase wiring across Dart services, Cloud Functions, rules, Android/iOS config files, and found critical iOS auth and rules hardening gaps.

critical.issue: Security rules allow anonymous users to create/update/delete watchlist and watched docs because they only check request.auth != null and userID match. This bypasses app-layer guest block and violates the requirement to block anonymous saves.

minor.issue: enforceAppCheck is set to false, increasing abuse surface for callable endpoint.

integration_issues.issue: Info.plist is missing CFBundleURLTypes and REVERSED_CLIENT_ID URL scheme entry required for Google OAuth callback on iOS.

action_items: Update firestore.rules for watchlist/watched to reject anonymous provider (e.g., request.auth.token.firebase.sign_in_provider != 'anonymous').
Add iOS Google Sign-In URL scheme wiring in Info.plist (CFBundleURLTypes with REVERSED_CLIENT_ID from GoogleService-Info.plist).
Optionally enable App Check enforcement for tmdbCallable before production rollout.
Ensure TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN is configured in Functions environment; otherwise TMDB callable falls back to demo catalog behavior.
critical_code_review_comments: FirebaseBootstrap, AuthService, NotificationService, StorageService, AnalyticsService, and FunctionsService are wired to live Firebase SDKs (no mock auth service found).
Cloud Functions exports are in asia-south1 and syntax checks pass.
TMDB fallback/demo catalog path is still present and intentionally used when callable/token fails (MOCKED fallback behavior remains).
test_credentials: Reviewed /app/memory/test_credentials.md: Google and anonymous auth flows are documented; no seeded email/password accounts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Flutter/Dart SDK unavailable in container, so no widget/integration runtime tests executed. Static checks performed for Dart/JS/plist/json and architecture wiring. Prior iteration_1 security concerns around proxy were already addressed in current functions/index.js allowlist + rate-limit logic.
rca of the issue: RCA-1 (iOS Google login): OAuth callback cannot return to app because Info.plist lacks CFBundleURLTypes/REVERSED_CLIENT_ID route. Reproduction: inspect /app/ios/Runner/Info.plist and verify those keys are absent. Mitigation: add URL types entry using reversed client ID from GoogleService-Info.plist. RCA-2 (anonymous writes): App blocks guest saves in LibraryService, but Firestore rules still permit anonymous users since request.auth exists for anonymous sessions. Reproduction: evaluate watchlist/watched rules conditions; no provider guard exists. Mitigation: add sign_in_provider != 'anonymous' checks on create/update/delete.
